text:
Founding (, Kessei) is the 38 th volume of the Hunter × Hunter manga series. It was released by Shueisha on September 4 th , 2024 in Japan and by Viz Media on January 6 th , 2026 in English.

Chapters

391. Clash: Part 2 (②, Shōtotsu (Ni))
392. Information (, Jōhō)
393. Plea (, Kongan)
394. Hypothesis (, Sōtei)
395. Founding: Part 1 (①, Kessei (Ichi))
396. Founding: Part 2 (②, Kessei (Ni))
397. Founding: Part 3 (③, Kessei (San))
398. Search (, Tansaku)
399. Expulsion (, Taikyo)
400. Secrecy (, Hitoku)

Summary

As Zakuro and Lynch begin to search for Hisoka on Tier 3 using the former's ability, Hinrigh uses his ability to secretly record a hallway in search of members of the Heil-Ly Family. He is then attacked by three Heil-Ly members but manages to quickly kill one of them and scare the other two away. Hinrigh then takes up the offer of Corporal Maizan, who has information on the location of the Heil-Ly's secret hideout. Zakuro and Lynch eventually find someone who appears to be Hisoka, with the man knocking Lynch unconscious as she tries to use her ability on him. Elsewhere, Tsudonke continues his quest to buy autograph paper so that he can receive signatures from the Phantom Troupe. As Ken'i addresses several Cha-R members, he worries about the situation with Hisoka, the Heil-Ly, and the Troupe.

After killing several Cha-R members at the family's office on Tier 5, Luini confronts Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan, gleefully hoping that they will help him destroy the world. However, Nobunaga swiftly kills him with his katana and declares that the Troupe will crush the Heil-Ly all on their own. Back on Tier 3, Zakuro is woken up by Lynch after being knocked unconscious, remembering that Hisoka was on his way to see a movie. He then meets up with Hinrigh and several other Xi-Yu Family members outside a cineplex, telling Hinrigh that he and Lynch will look for the Troupe's leader and ask for the destruction of the Heil-Ly in exchange for Hisoka's location. In one of the theaters, Hinrigh makes a deal with Hisoka and asks that he wait on Tier 1 until the Heil-Ly are eliminated, giving him a card to a room in the VVIP area. Elsewhere on Tier 3, Ken'i and Hinrigh work with Maizan to investigate the Heil-Ly's trapped room. In the Heil-Ly's hideout, the group's members continue to level up, and Morena gives instructions to each of them. On Tier 3, several soldiers (all of whom are friends with Prince Tserriednich) break into the Heil-Ly's office in search of Morena so that they can capture her without troubling the Prince. After the soldiers discuss the situation and Nen in detail, Borksen suggests that they avoid Morena at all costs while pretending to look for her.

After Hinrigh sees that the Heil-Ly members are most likely using a teleportation ability to enter their hideout, he and Ken'i discuss the situation with the aforementioned soldiers, deciding to have the Troupe investigate the trap. While Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan are investigating, the resignation and anger of the Heil-Ly members reminds Nobunaga of the founding of the Troupe. In a flashback at Meteor City, a young Chrollo is shown working with Pakunoda, Sheila, and Sarasa to dub videotapes of the Mighty Sweepin' Power Cleaners. After technical difficulties during the screening, the four voice the characters live on stage in front of all the other children, leaving them stunned by the performance. With Chrollo's other friends joining them, the group continues to perform live on stage and decides to call itself a troupe. Soon after, Sarasa is abducted and killed by unknown perpetrators, with the Troupe members finding her body in a nearby forest and then laying her to rest. With the exception of Sheila, all the members follow Chrollo's plan to avenge her death and protect Meteor City, unanimously choosing Chrollo as their leader.

Back in the present, as the three Troupe members analyze the trapped room and prepare to enter the Heil-Ly's hideout, Ken'i introduces them to Hinrigh, who volunteers to enter first, swallowing a transmitter and giving them the receiver to track his location. Nobunaga decides to follow after him, and the two confront the Heil-Ly members in their hideout. However, they are quickly overwhelmed by Terebellum and Yokotani's abilities, with Nobunaga losing his katana before being expelled. Hinrigh then manages to regurgitate and hide the transmitter before also being sent back to the trapped room. Using the receiver, the three Troupe members determine that the Heil-Ly's hideout is on Tier 2. On Tier 1, Izunavi asks Prince Tyson to convince her mother to ask the King at the next banquet to read the Book of Tyson, hoping that it can influence him to end the succession contest.

In the interrogation rooms at the Justice Bureau, Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast (having taken Kacho's form) explains the situation to a distraught Melody. After discussing the situation with him, the two agree to work with Kaiser, who expresses his love for Melody and proposes killing the higher-ranking Princes with a slow-acting poison at the next banquet. In the VIP Witness Protection Area, Kacho finds a delirious and exhausted Fugetsu, who reveals that she can use her Magical Worm ability all by herself and multiple times a day. After Fugetsu passes out, Kacho asks Melody for help, and the two have Kaiser adjust the meeting times so that Melody can pass Fugetsu in the halls. Melody sees flies buzzing around her and multiple evil spirits attached to her, telling Kaiser that they should hold off on their plan since they don't know which Prince is behind Fugetsu's condition. Melody asks to be let out early, but after Kaiser explains why she needs to be kept safe in custody, Melody asks him to visit Kurapika and to tell him everything they know, continuing to worry about the situation and left unable to rule out any of the Princes or their people as suspects. In Room 1014, Longhi reveals to Kurapika and Bill that she can already use Nen, and Kurapika agrees to collaborate with Prince Tubeppa.
